text:
Ponzu (ポンズ, Ponzu) was one of the 24 examinees that managed to enter the Fourth Phase of the 287th Hunter Exam. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 3, Chapter 23 She was examinee #246 in the 287th Hunter Exam and #233 Hunter × Hunter - Episode 66 (2011) in the 288th Hunter Exam. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 148 

Appearance

Ponzu wore a big yellow hat with white flaps that housed bees that would come out at her will or when she fell or screamed. She wore a salmon blouse, pale yellow pants, and orange flats. In the 1999 anime adaptation, she wore a pink hat, a pink jacket with blue sleeves, yellow baggy pants, and blue shoes.

Personality

Ponzu was a serious, generally collected individual. Despite indirectly causing Bourbon's death, she showed no sign of feeling guilt, since the attack had been accidental. She was quite perceptive, as she discovered the transmitter installed in her tag. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 4, Chapter 31 She also was very objective, characterized by her prioritization of getting word of the Chimera Ant infestation to a strong Hunter. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 19, Chapter 190 

Plot

Hunter Exam arc

Ponzu enters the 287th Hunter Exam and completes the first three phases of the Hunter Exam with relative ease. As an examinee who passed the Third Phase, she receives an unconditional invitation to the 288th Hunter Exam should she fail the 287th Hunter Exam. Sometime during the Fourth Phase (where examinees seek to obtain the badges of their targets), Ponzu learns that Bourbon is her target. Ponzu stalks Bourbon and eventually sees him enter a cave. She shoots sleeping gas into the cave and waits for it to take effect before entering. She finds Bourbon asleep, but finds out about the precautions he managed to set when she drew close to his body to search for his badge. Bourbon's snakes drop from crevices overhead where the gas did not reach, having been set up to attack anyone that approaches him or tries to leave the cave. Ponzu manages to jump back before touching him and so was not attacked, but this sudden motion triggers Ponzu's own defenses, a mass of bees hiding in her clothing. These bees sting Bourbon, who unexpectedly has an allergic reaction to the stings and dies, trapping Ponzu in the cave with the now deceased Bourbon. Ponzu deems herself a failure in her hunt and resigns herself to wait for the Hunter Exam committee to rescue her using the transmitter in her badge. 

Sometime during the Fourth Phase, Tonpa identifies Ponzu as Leorio's target for him in exchange for Leorio's medical assistance. Tonpa also shares Ponzu's features, weapons, and combat style with Leorio. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 3, Chapter 25 How Tonpa obtained the information is unknown. Using Gon's sense of smell, Leorio manages to find the cave where Ponzu is waiting to be rescued and makes his way in. Ponzu warns Leorio about the snake trap, but Leorio insists on running to the exit to warn Gon and Kurapika and gets bitten by the snakes as a result. Gon and Kurapika rush in to find Leorio, and are similarly warned by Ponzu about Bourbon's snakes. Ponzu also tells Kurapika about Bourbon's death, after Kurapika tries to negotiate with him to release the trap, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 4, Chapter 30 and about the trap itself. Ponzu initially chooses to share only the fact that she killed Bourbon, but later confirms Kurapika's theory that it was due to her neurotoxic bees. She also shares how she and Bourbon ended up trapped in the cave with Gon and Kurapika. 

Gon later decides to search Bourbon's body for his badge and an antidote to his snake's venom despite Ponzu's warning. Ponzu sees this and tries to warn him again, but Gon does so anyway. She is shocked to see Gon let himself get bitten despite her multiple warnings, and risk his life despite not knowing if Bourbon had an antidote on him. Ponzu accepts Gon's offer to have her spray sleeping gas to make the snakes sleep and lets herself be rescued by Gon. At Gon's signal she sprays the sleeping gas and is carried out with the other two sleeping examinees by Gon. Gon carries them out of the cave, but steals Ponzu's badge to give to Leorio. Likely as a result of this, Ponzu ends up without the required number of points to pass the Fourth Phase of the 287th Hunter Exam, and her journey ends there. 

Greed Island arc

Ponzu is shown to participate in the 288th Hunter Exam, although she then ends up being knocked unconscious in the First Phase by Killua. 

Chimera Ant arc

Ponzu infiltrates NGL with Pokkle and two friends of theirs (Balda and Pekuba). They come upon a group of Chimera Ants capturing and killing humans, and she suffers a minor injury to her shoulder, but the four manage to escape. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 19, Chapter 189 Ponzu sends her bees to contact the five other Hunter groups they had established a network with, to no avail. They conclude that they cannot contain the Chimera Ants by themselves and resolve to contact the outside world. However, before they get the chance, Zazan's Squad detects them. Pokkle orders Ponzu to escape, and she is the only one who manages to do so. Knowing that Pokkle will not be eaten immediately, she rushes to write SOS messages in blood and uses her bees to bring them to the strongest Hunter nearby—who happens to be Kite. She then decides to run back to the border, but she is shot moments later by an officer from Zazan's Squad, who then devours her. 

Equipment

Soporific Gas: Ponzu used a vaporizer spraying potent sleeping gas to knock out her targets. In the Fourth Phase of the Hunter Exam, the gas has been seen on separate occasions knocking out Bourbon, his snakes, Leorio, and Kurapika. 

Beehive Hat: Ponzu's hat is inhabited by many bees. If she screams or falls due to a shock, the bees will come out and attack everyone around her without discrimination. Upon stinging, they inject a paralyzing poison that caused Bourbon a lethal anaphylactic shock, since he had an allergy to them. Ponzu can lightly tap her hat and make her bees come out without attacking anyone. 

Abilities & Powers

Ponzu's primary weapons are traps using chemicals, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 3, Chapter 25 but she has shown to use other means of combat. She carries a beehive underneath her hat, and the bees emerge to attack her enemies when she is threatened. She seems to be fairly analytical, realizing the nature of Bourbon's ability and that the badges used in the Fourth Phase of the Hunter Exam have transmitters implanted in them. In the 2011 anime adaptation, Ponzu was also extremely confident that Leorio would not be able to beat her, and stated that Leorio would need a miracle to win. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 17 (2011) 

Preternatural Perception: According to Tonpa, she can quickly discern when she is being followed. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 4, Chapter 30 

Advanced Speed: Ponzu ran away from the Chimera Ants relatively quickly, although she wasn't fast enough to dodge a bullet. 

Advanced Agility: Having passed the amended Second Phase of the Hunter Exam, Ponzu proved more nimble than many other applicants. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 2, Chapter 12 

Advanced Stamina: Ponzu was able to run almost 80 km without rest during the First Phase of the Hunter Exam, showing a noteworthy degree of fitness. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 7 

Nen

Ponzu was a Manipulator leaning toward Emission. She learned Nen sometime before sneaking into NGL, as she was able to see Pokkle's Nen ability and seemingly developed her Nen ability focused on manipulating bees with Nen rather than basic animal control. 

Manga Appearances

Battles

Trivia
 
Her name probably comes from ponzu, a citrus-based sauce commonly used in Japanese cuisine.
Ponzu and Pokkle are the only deceased examinees of the 287th Hunter Exam who did not die during the exam.
The symbol in the S.O.S. message sent by Ponzu, 〒, is the Japanese postal mark written before postal codes that also denotes a post office in a map.
Ponzu is the only participant of the Fourth Phase of the 287th Hunter Exam to finish with exactly 3 points. This disqualifies her from continuing to the Final Phase.

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 2011 anime adaptation, a scene is added after the 287th Hunter Exam finishes, where Gon, Kurapika, and Leorio run through traffic to make their flight to the Republic of Padokea. Ponzu is seen in the front seat of one of the cars stuck in traffic. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 21 (2011) 

The 2011 anime adaptation expands Ponzu's appearances during the Chimera Ant arc: she is first seen with Pokkle and his friends in a park discussing the attack of giant insects in NGL. At first, she wasn't allowed to go along, but she proved that she would help relay messages due to her ability to control her bees. At one point, she is chased by Peons, whom Pokkle kills. After her death, her remains are found by Kite, Gon, and Killua. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 80 (2011) 
In the 1999 anime adaptation, Ponzu is seen with Pokkle trying to get a signal from the radio during the extra 3rd phase. She is one of the applicants who chose to stay and work as a team with the others to reach Zevil Island.

Intertextuality and References

Her hat looks similar to Koenma's from YuYu Hakusho.

In the 2011 anime adaptation, Ponzu's death in the hands of the Gun-toting Ant looks strikingly similar to a scene in episode 535 of the Detective Conan series when a detective got shot. Curiously, the two scenes are both exclusive to the anime adaptation of their respective title while not being present or shown in the manga.

Miscellaneous

In the first character popularity poll, Ponzu placed 9th with 294 votes.
In the third character popularity poll, Ponzu placed 20th with 108 votes.
Ponzu shares the same voice actress as Kortopi of the Phantom Troupe as well as Anita and Zushi, in the 1999 anime adaptation.
